div1.entryFree:
Rock, vb. 1) tr. to move to and fro as in a cradle, to lull asleep: my throbbing heart shall r. thee day and night, Ven. 1186. take hands with me and r. the ground whereon these sleepers be, Mids. IV, 1, 91. death r. me asleep, H4B II, 4, 211. r. his brains in cradle of the rude imperious surge, III, 1, 19. sleep r. thy brain, Hml. III, 2, 237. if drink r. not his cradle, Oth. II, 3, 136.
2) intr. to shake, to tremble: and then it (her hand) faster --ed, Lucr. 262.
